description: Feeling deep pleasure or satisfaction in one's achievements or qualities; having appropriate self-respect. 'Phoru' names the quality of healthy pride—dignity without arrogance, satisfaction without superiority.
sound_symbolism: The word stands tall. 'Pho' /ɸo/ begins with grounded breath, dignity present. Then 'ru' rolls with quiet confidence, pride embodied.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'phoru miona' (proud person). Phi pride is not arrogance—it is right relationship with one's worth and achievements.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Healthy pride acknowledges genuine achievement without inflating self or diminishing others.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Proud communication expresses satisfaction without comparison—celebrating self without competing.
